zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard FitzGilbert de Saint Valéry於從1005年到1037年出生在法國諾門迪瑟-馬利沙吉瓦萊--考時，他的父親Gilbert I de Saint Valery60歲，母親Papia de Normandy58歲。他於1029年與Ada d'Hugleville在法國諾門迪瑟-馬利福馬賀結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於從1054年到1103年在法國諾門迪瑟-馬利格列維爾--考。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard FitzGilbert de Saint Valéry于从1005年到1037年出生在法国諾門迪瑟-馬利沙吉瓦萊--考时，他的父亲Gilbert I de Saint Valery60岁，母亲Papia de Normandy58岁。他于1029年与Ada d'Hugleville在法国諾門迪瑟-馬利福馬賀结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于从1054年到1103年在法国諾門迪瑟-馬利格列維爾--考。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Saint
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cyr, Remy.

English and French: nickname for a particularly pious individual, from Old French and Middle English saint(e), seint(e) ‘holy’ (from Latin sanctus ‘blameless, holy’). The vocabulary word was occasionally used in the Middle Ages as a personal name and this may have given rise to some instances of the surname.
English: perhaps a nickname for someone who wore, made, or sold girdles, from Middle English ceint, seint, saint ‘girdle’ (Old French ceint, Latin cinctum). Compare Center .

variants.name: Center
meanings: English and Scottish (Aberdeenshire): metonymic occupational name from Middle English, Old French ceinture, seinture ‘girdle’, denoting someone who made or sold girdles, or perhaps a nickname for someone who wore a distinctive girdle.
English: variant of   Senter   1.
Americanized form of German   Zehnder   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Sant
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Krishan, Anagha, Anjali, Arvind, Deepak, Milind, Nilima, Ravindra, Sanjeev, Sanjiv, Vasudev.
English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Saint  .
Italian (northeastern): variant of   Santo  .

variants.name: Sante
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Luc, Camille, Lucien.  Spanish Alejandro, Anselmo, Carlos, Emilio, Jesusa, Jorge.
French (also Santé): probably a nickname for a healthy person, from French santé ‘good health’.

Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Spanish (southwestern): habitational name from one of several places called Sante, in Galicia, Portugal, and Asturias, from a Visigothic personal name. This surname is also found in the Philippines.

nameMeanings.name: Valéry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Colette, Pierre, Raoul, Yva.
French and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the personal name Valery, French form of Latin Valerius (see Valerio ).
Americanized form of Italian   Valerio   or   Valeri  .

variants.name: Vallery
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Leonce, Monique.
French:
habitational name from Vallery in Yonne, once a Gallo-Roman estate, recorded in 1218 as Valerianus.

variants.name: Valerio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Ana, Manuel, Pedro, Mario, Luis, Francisco, Ramon, Ruben, Carlos, Jesus.  Italian Antonio, Angelo, Lucio, Francesco, Maurizio, Niccolo, Romeo, Vito, Amadeo, Anastasio, Aureliano, Carmela.

Spanish and Italian: from the personal name Valerio, from Latin Valerius, a Roman surname ultimately derived from Latin valere ‘to flourish, to be strong and healthy’. The name was borne by several early Christian saints, among them 4th-century bishops of Trier and Zaragoza and 5th-century bishops of Sorrento and of Antibes. Compare Valery .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard FitzGilbert de Saint Valéry was born from 1005 to 1037, in Saint-Valéry-en-Caux, Seine-Maritime, Normandy, France, his father, Gilbert I de Saint Valery, was 60 and his mother, Papia de Normandy, was 58. He married Ada d'Hugleville in 1029, in Neufmarché, Seine-Maritime, Normandy, France. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died from 1054 to 1103, in Hugleville-en-Caux, Seine-Maritime, Normandy, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard FitzGilbert de Saint Valéry est né du 1005 au 1037, à Saint-Valéry-en-Caux, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, France, son père, Gilbert I de Saint Valery, avait 60 ans et sa mère, Papia de Normandy, avait 58 ans. Il a épousé Ada d'Hugleville en 1029, à Neufmarché, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé du 1054 au 1103, à Hugleville-en-Caux, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard FitzGilbert de Saint Valéry von 1005 bis 1037 in Saint-Valéry-en-Caux, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gilbert I de Saint Valery 60 und seine Mutter Papia de Normandy 58. Er hat 1029 in Neufmarché, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Frankreich Ada d'Hugleville geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb von 1054 bis 1103 in Hugleville-en-Caux, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard FitzGilbert de Saint Valéry è nato il da 1005 a 1037 nel Saint-Valéry-en-Caux, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, Gilbert I de Saint Valery, aveva 60 anni e il suo madre, Papia de Normandy, aveva 58 anni. Lui sposato Ada d'Hugleville nel 1029, nel Neufmarché, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì da 1054 a 1103, nel Hugleville-en-Caux, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard FitzGilbert de Saint Valéryがから1005年まで1037年にフランスノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメサイント-ヴァルeリ-エン-カウックスで生まれた時，父Gilbert I de Saint Valeryは60歳，母Papia de Normandyは58歳であった。1029年にフランスノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメネウフマルチeでAda d'Huglevilleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼はから1054年まで1103年にフランスノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメハグレヴィレ-エン-カウックスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard FitzGilbert de Saint Valéry(이)가 1005년 부터 1037년 까지 프랑스 노먼디 세이인-마이팀 세인트-발e리--카스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gilbert I de Saint Valery(은)는 60세였고, 그의 어머니 Papia de Normandy(은)는 58세였다. 그는 1029년 프랑스 노먼디 세이인-마이팀 니우프마che에서 Ada d'Hugleville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1054년 부터 1103년 까지 프랑스 노먼디 세이인-마이팀 휴글레비레--카스에서 사망했다.

lv.from 1005 to 1037–from 1054 to 1103: no 1005. gada līdz 1037. gadam–no 1054. gada līdz 1103. gadam

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard FitzGilbert de Saint Valéry nasceu de 1005 a 1037, em Saint-Valéry-en-Caux, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, França, seu pai, Gilbert I de Saint Valery, tinha 60 anos e sua mãe, Papia de Normandy, tinha 58 anos. Ele casou-se com Ada d'Hugleville em 1029, em Neufmarché, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu de 1054 a 1103, em Hugleville-en-Caux, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard FitzGilbert de Saint Valéry родился с 1005 по 1037 в местности сеинт-валeри-ен-каукс, сине-маритим, норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Gilbert I de Saint Valery было 60 лет, а матери, которую звали Papia de Normandy, 58. Он женился на женщине по имени Ada d'Hugleville в 1029 г. в местности неуфмарчe, сине-маритим, норманди, Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер с 1054 по 1103 в местности хуглевилле-ен-каукс, сине-маритим, норманди, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard FitzGilbert de Saint Valéry nació desde 1005 hasta 1037, en Saint-Valéry-en-Caux, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia, su papá, Gilbert I de Saint Valery, tenía 60 años y su mamá, Papia de Normandy, tenía 58 años. Se casó con Ada d'Hugleville en 1029, en Neufmarché, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió desde 1054 hasta 1103, en Hugleville-en-Caux, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia.
